title: After-Action Report — Ballparks, Named Managers, Arsenal Chips, Handedness Splits, Pitch-Type Matchup, Batted-Ball Physics
scope: Open-ended continuation of the prior session. Items piled on over the course of the conversation: handedness UI, arsenal chips, handedness splits, ballparks, pitch-type matchup in pick_new_pitcher(), named managers with fog-of-war ratings, batted-ball physics (hybrid layer). Final ask: AAR covering the whole batch.

rationale.item: bats / throws were already persisted and consumed by the engine but invisible on the box score and team page
User asked for the hybrid approach specifically: keep the categorical contact engine canonical, layer EV/LA/spray for visualization only

A full physics rewrite would have invalidated the existing engine calibration AND required an O27-specific probability surface (MLB Statcast data is wrong for the 12-batter / 27-out / sidearm structure).

delegation_events.quote: No flavor tags ("Bandbox", "Pitcher's Park"). User ruled out.
generate dimensions ("what does the ideal O27 ballpark look like? idk!")

delegation_events.decision: Hybrid batted-ball layer: categorical engine canonical, EV/LA/spray for visualization only
delegation_events.quote: User asked for the hybrid approach specifically: keep the categorical contact engine canonical, layer EV/LA/spray for visualization only.

delegation_events.decision: Manager tactical axes shown with customer-facing labels (fog of war)
delegation_events.quote: render the eight tactical axes with customer-facing labels (fog of war) so the underlying mechanism stays hidden, video-game-style

actions.description: Handedness UI: box score shows (L)/(R)/(S) after batter names and (LHP)/(RHP) after pitchers; team page B/T and T columns

Ballparks: new teams columns park_name, park_dimensions (JSON), manager_name; _roll_ballpark_name (o27v2/league.py:777-820) with templated names from regional surname pools; _roll_park_dimensions with asymmetry + wall-height heavy tail; Ballpark card on team page; box-score header renders 'at <Park Name>'

Named managers + fog-of-war ratings: per-team named manager via mgr_name_picker in seed_league; Manager card rewritten with name + archetype and eight 5-dot axes (Quick Hook · Bullpen Touch · Reads the Moment · Wild Card Energy · Matchup Hunter · Plays the Splits · Greenlight · Plays the Bench); new Jinja filter rating_stars
Arsenal chips: new Jinja filter repertoire mapping quality floats to 20-80 scout grades with tiers and humanized pitch names; pitcher page renders tier-colored chips sorted by usage_weight
Handedness splits: SQL helpers _player_handedness_split_batter/_pitcher aggregating game_pa_log by opposing handedness; new Handedness Splits panel on player page

Pitch-type matchup in pick_new_pitcher: _matchup_bonus reads next 3 batters' Power/Eye/handedness, scores k_delta, hard_contact_shift, opposite_heavy weighted by usage_weight × quality; scaled by mgr_platoon_aggression, capped ±0.12

Batted-ball physics hybrid layer: sample_batted_ball in new o27/engine/batted_ball.py (~110 lines); three new game_pa_log columns (exit_velocity, launch_angle, spray_angle); wiring in prob.py / render.py / sim.py; Spray Charts SVG panel on game page with _bip_distance_ft heuristic

risk_posture: Schema changes (additive only) ... All migrations idempotent. Fresh-DB SCHEMA blocks updated to match.
The engine never reads the new fields; they're flavor-and-charts only.
No new gameplay dials. Dimensions are visualization-only. park_hr / park_hits remain the mechanical multipliers.
The internal mechanism (`mgr_quick_hook` 0.78, etc.) is unchanged. The manager AI keeps reading the raw floats — only the display layer is reframed.
it can never override a real Stuff / Stamina / rest gap

validation_claims.claim: Handedness ratios match design intent on a fresh seed: bats 54.3% R / 34.2% L / 11.4% S vs design 55 / 33 / 12; throws 73.2% R / 26.8% L vs design 70 / 30
validation_claims.check_method: Seed a league and aggregate bats/throws distributions

validation_claims.claim: Sanity check on a 10-game sample: EV by quality weak μ 70.9 / medium μ 85.9 / hard μ 103.1; LA by hit_type ground_out μ -5°, single μ +14°, double μ +22°, triple μ +18°, hr μ +29°, fly_out μ +38°
validation_claims.check_method: Sim 10 games and aggregate EV/LA from game_pa_log

validation_claims.claim: Test client hit on /game/<id> returned 200, SVG present, panel rendered
validation_claims.check_method: Hit /game/<id> with Flask test client and check for spray-chart SVG

validation_claims.claim: Sri Lankan-named "Hasaranga Ground" actually showed up in a sample seed
validation_claims.check_method: Seed with a Sri Lankan regional preset and inspect park names

non_actions.item: New gameplay dials from park dimensions
non_actions.reason: Dimensions are visualization-only. park_hr / park_hits remain the mechanical multipliers.

non_actions.item: Full physics rewrite (EV/LA driving the fielding outcome)
non_actions.reason: Per the prior AAR's backlog plan: this is the hybrid layer. Full physics rewrite ... is still deferred.

non_actions.item: Switch hitters in pitcher handedness splits
non_actions.reason: the engine doesn't resolve their effective side per AB

residual_risks: Handedness splits are BIP-only. K / BB / HBP outcomes don't appear in `game_pa_log` today, so the vs LHP / vs RHP split misses the K rate and walk rate.
Batted-ball physics is decorative. Engine fielding outcomes are still the categorical model.
Distance heuristic is non-physical. `_bip_distance_ft()` is a visual approximation.
Spray chart doesn't respect park dimensions. Every park's SVG uses the same 400-ft arc and ±45° foul lines.
Switch hitters are excluded from pitcher handedness splits.
Manager pitch-type matchup isn't surfaced — it's just a hidden bonus on top of the existing score.
No EV/LA/spray export. The plaintext box-score and markdown export still emit categorical hit_type only.
open_hooks: The renderer would need to append pa_log rows for terminal-K / terminal-BB / terminal-HBP events to close the splits gap
A real projectile-physics distance would let the spray chart respect actual park dimensions and produce robbed-homer / wall-scraper narratives
Drawing the actual fence shape from park_dimensions
PITCH_CATALOG's hard_contact_shift and platoon_mode as one source of truth for both the matchup bonus and EV sampler
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — Ballparks, Named Managers, Arsenal Chips, Handedness Splits, Pitch-Type Matchup, Batted-Ball Physics
unmapped_content: Header metadata: Branch claude/review-and-improve-NbhPy. | Sampler detail: EV distributions per contact quality (weak μ74 σ7.5 [52,88]; medium μ88 σ6 [76,100]; hard μ102 σ5.5 [92,119]); hit-type EV nudges (HR +4 mph, infield_single -3.5); batter Power ±10 mph; pitch hard_contact_shift up to ~1.5 mph; LA per hit_type; spray gaussian around pull skew (RHB μ-12°, LHB μ+12°, switch μ0°, σ16, clamped ±44°). | Files-changed table with line counts. | Honest limitation flagged in the splits panel header: game_pa_log captures BIP events only, so these read as 'contact production vs L/R-handed opposition'. | Process notes: ballpark scope shrank after pushback and manager work was added mid-migration — adapted by extending the same migration (one ALTER TABLE per column, not two passes); 5-dot scale chosen over stars (reads as rating bar, not review score); hybrid-physics was the right scope (<300 lines); tripped up by _stat_delta whitelist again — assists work earlier this session had the same pattern; EV/LA work avoided it because it lives entirely on game_pa_log.
parser_notes: Multi-feature batch AAR; 'Honest gaps' items 5-7 overlap non_actions/residual_risks and were recorded under residual_risks.
